steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states directly: authorship of No. 62 is hard to determine ‘not from a lack of evidence but from too much contradictory evidence.’ The correct choice must reflect this specific reason.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'strong cases exist for either author': contradictory evidence pointing to both candidates is exactly why determination is difficult. ✓
  • Choice B — 'separate versions were created': the passage never mentions separate versions. This is an invented detail. ✗
  • Choice C — 'evidence is missing': the passage explicitly says the problem is not a lack of evidence. This directly contradicts the text. ✗
  • Choice D — 'the two authors collaborated': the either-or framing in the passage does not suggest collaboration. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
